title: Functions That Give Back
description: Learn how functions can return data - enabling powerful data processing and calculations.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: So far our functions have performed actions. But functions can also calculate and return values!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Return values? Like giving back an answer?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! A function can do calculations and 'return' the result so you can use it elsewhere in your code.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So functions can both DO things and CALCULATE things?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Perfect understanding! Let's create a function that returns a value.

phases.title: Your First Return Statement
phases.description: Return a value from a function

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Use the 'return' keyword to send a value back. Try: def get_wheat_price(): return 10

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So this function returns the number 10?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! When you call get_wheat_price(), you get back the value 10.

objectives.description: Create a function with a return statement

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! Your function now produces a value instead of just performing actions.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: To use a return value, store it in a variable: price = get_wheat_price(). The variable will hold the returned value!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So the function's return value gets assigned to the variable?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! The function runs, calculates its result, returns it, and that value goes into your variable.

objectives.description: Call a function and store its return value in a variable
Execute your code to see return values in action

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: The variable now holds the value my function returned!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! This is how functions pass data around in your program.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Functions can calculate before returning! Try: def calculate_value(wheat_count): return wheat_count * 10

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So it takes wheat_count as input, multiplies by 10, and returns the result?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! Functions can process input and return output. They're like little calculation machines!

objectives.description: Create a function that calculates and returns a value
Use multiplication in your return statement

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: Functions as calculation tools! This opens up so many possibilities!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Absolutely! Return values let functions communicate with each other and share data. Next, let's build a whole library of farming functions!

rewards.description: Unlocked quest: Building Your Function Toolbox
